_comment: Quick coding-task workload. Self-contained — ships its own minimal Python sandbox via setupContent, so it runs out of the box with no external project setup. Pairs the chat-completion smoke (`quick-q`) with a tool-call smoke at the same scale. Verifies that the dispatched role can read a file, identify a bug, edit the file, and confirm via a test run. Uses Python's stdlib unittest so no pip install is required on a fresh Mac.

workload.description: Minimal coding-task smoke. Agent reads a buggy Python function, fixes it, confirms the unittest passes. Runs out of the box on any machine with python3 (Mac default). Uses the `coder` role (allowed to edit) — pairing with `code-reviewer` is a role/task mismatch since reviewers are forbidden from modifying code by their doctrine.
workload.prompt:
There is a bug in ${SANDBOX_DIR}/bug.py. The function `add_positive(a, b)` is supposed to return the sum of two positive integers, but it returns the difference instead.

Fix the bug. Constraints:

- Modify ONLY ${SANDBOX_DIR}/bug.py.
- Do NOT modify ${SANDBOX_DIR}/test_bug.py.
- The fix should preserve the existing ValueError on non-positive inputs.
- After your edit, the unittest must pass:
    cd ${SANDBOX_DIR} && python3 -m unittest test_bug.py

Reply with a one-line summary of the fix.
